description_long: This object consists of five distinct residential houses arranged in a row, each with unique architectural features. The houses display a mix of roof types, including gable, hip, and flat roofs, and vary in size and layout. The exteriors are primarily finished in neutral tones such as gray, white, and black, with some houses featuring porches and overhangs. Windows are arranged in both symmetrical and asymmetrical patterns, with some houses having large front-facing windows and others with smaller, more regularly spaced windows. Entryways are visible on several houses, some with steps and covered porches, while others have simpler, flush entrances. The overall composition suggests a suburban neighborhood setting with a variety of modern and traditional design elements.
description: A group of five residential houses with varied roof styles, neutral exteriors, and diverse window and entryway arrangements, evoking a suburban neighborhood.
description_view_0: Front view showing the facades of all five houses, highlighting porches, entryways, and the diversity in roof shapes and window arrangements.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of each house, the flatness of some roofs, and the staggered alignment of the buildings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back sides of the houses, with less architectural detail and more focus on the roof structures.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the internal layouts and room divisions, with visible wall separations and fewer exterior features.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with a mix of large and small windows
attribute_entryway_design: Varied; some with covered porches and steps, others with simple flush doors and minimal overhangs
